segments.speaker: NICK
segments.en: Good morning, and welcome to Oniton Hall, one of the largest estates in the area.
segments.zh: 早上好，欢迎来到奥尼顿庄园，这是该地区最大的庄园之一。我叫尼克，是这里的导游之一。大家先坐着，我简单介绍一下庄园，然后我们再四处走走。

segments.speaker: NICK
segments.en: My name's Nick, and I'm one of the guides.

segments.speaker: NICK
segments.en: I'll give you a brief introduction to the estate while you're sitting down, and then we'll walk round.

segments.speaker: NICK
segments.en: The estate consists of the house, gardens, parkland and farm, and it dates back to the fourteenth century.

segments.speaker: NICK
segments.en: The original house was replaced in the late seventeenth century, and of course it has had a large number of owners.

segments.speaker: NICK
segments.en: Almost all of them have left their mark, generally by adding new rooms, like the ballroom and conservatory, or by demolishing others.

segments.speaker: NICK
segments.en: The farm looks much as it's always done, although the current owner has done a great deal of work to the flower beds.

segments.speaker: NICK
segments.en: In the seventeenth century, the estate was owned by a very wealthy man called Sir Edward Downes.
segments.zh: 17世纪时，这座庄园属于一位非常富有的人，名叫爱德华·唐斯爵士。在多年活跃于政坛之后，他想要远离政治世界，建造一座配得上他大量藏书、绘画和雕塑收藏的新房子。他与以前的政治盟友断绝了联系，并主办了画家、诗人等创意和文学人士的聚会。在他那个时代，他不关心客人是富是穷，只要有才华就行。

segments.speaker: NICK
segments.en: His intention was to escape from the world of politics, after years as an active politician, and to build a new house worthy of his big collection of books, paintings and sculptures.

segments.speaker: NICK
segments.en: He broke off contact with his former political allies, and hosted meetings of creative and literary people, like painters and poets.

segments.speaker: NICK
segments.en: Unusually for his time, he didn't care whether his guests were rich or poor, as long as they had talent.

segments.speaker: NICK
segments.en: Big houses like Oniton had dozens of servants until the 1920s or 30s, and we've tried to show what their working lives were like.
segments.zh: 像奥尼顿这样的大房子，直到20世纪20年代或30年代都有几十名仆人，我们试图展示他们的工作生活是什么样的。照片当然不能提供太多信息，所以取而代之的是，当你参观房子时，你会看到志愿者打扮成19世纪的仆人，正在做他们的工作。他们会解释他们在做什么，告诉你他们的食谱，或者他们正在使用的工具。我们刚刚引入这个特色，以取代我们以前提供的语音导览。

segments.speaker: NICK
segments.en: Photographs of course don't give much of an idea, so instead, as you go round the house, you'll see volunteers dressed up as nineteenth-century servants, going about their work.

segments.speaker: NICK
segments.en: They'll explain what they're doing, and tell you their recipes, or what tools they're using.

segments.speaker: NICK
segments.en: We've just introduced this feature to replace the audio guide we used to have available.

items.prompt: Many past owners made changes to

pairs.note: past owners 对应 a large number of owners，都是指过去的拥有者。
made changes to 对应 have left their mark，意思是留下印记，即做出改变。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问过去许多主人对庄园做了什么改变，定位句提到大量主人留下了印记，通过添加或拆除房间等方式，因此答案选B。

items.prompt: Sir Edward Downes built Oniton Hall because he wanted

pairs.q: built Oniton Hall because he wanted
pairs.note: 题干中的'because he wanted'对应原文中的'His intention was to'，表示目的。

pairs.q: escape from the world of politics
pairs.p: escape from the world of politics
pairs.note: 题干中的'escape from the world of politics'与原文完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干中的'because he wanted'找到原文中的'His intention was to'，后面直接给出了他的目的。

items.prompt: Visitors can learn about the work of servants in the past from

pairs.q: learn about the work of servants
pairs.p: show what their working lives were like
pairs.note: 'learn about' 对应 'show what...were like'，都是了解的意思。
'in the past' 被具体化为 'nineteenth-century'，表示过去的时间。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问游客可以从什么了解过去仆人的工作，定位句提到 'we've tried to show what their working lives were like' 和 'volunteers dressed up as nineteenth-century servants'，说明是通过志愿者扮演的仆人了解，对应选项C。

items.prompt: What is new for children at Oniton Hall?
